text.title: GDPR DSAR Runbook — Product Dev Team Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed when gdpr-dsar-runbook-product-dev-team is applied incorrectly. Each entry has a symptom (`description`), a root cause (`reason`), and a corrective fix (in `note`).

antipattern.description: Engineer dumps raw DB rows to subject; third-party data in `referrer_user_id` column.
antipattern.reason: No redaction policy; engineer treated request as a single SQL query.
antipattern.note: Fix: Mandatory redaction-pass step; second-pair-of-eyes review.

antipattern.description: Identity verified via the same email the request came from; attacker took over account 24h prior.
antipattern.note: Fix: Use independent evidence (transaction ID, prior support ticket, ID document for high-risk).

antipattern.description: DSAR delivered Day 47 with no extension notice.
antipattern.note: Fix: Ticketing automation: timer + alerts at Day 20/25/29.
